verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich protein expressed in the epididymis that binds to the sperm surface (postacrosomal/equatorial segment) and is involved in sperm-egg fusion. It is secreted into the epididymal lumen and associates stably with the sperm plasma membrane outer leaflet, making it surface-accessible on sperm cells. However, this surface display is restricted to a narrow germline/reproductive lineage (epididymal secretion onto sperm), qualifying it as tissue_restricted_surface rather than broadly expressed surface protein.
